title: NOC 40019 – Other Managers in Public Administration | GTR Immigration
description: Other Managers in Public Administration under NOC 40019 are senior-level leaders overseeing government units, intergovernmental affairs, legislative coordination, and specialized administrative functions across Canada. Classified as TEER 0 (Management Occupation), this role represents a high-skilled pathway within Canada’s public sector. As federal, provincial, and municipal governments continue expanding administrative reforms, intergovernmental collaboration, and public service modernization, experienced public administration managers remain in steady demand.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER0 – Management occupationsMajor Group40 – Managers in public administrationSub-Major Group400 – Managers in public administrationMinor Group4001 – Managers in public administrationNOC Code40019

common_job_titles: Clerk of the committee – Legislative Assembly
duties: Other Managers in Public Administration perform some or all of the following:
Advise senior officials on policy and program development
Establish procedures to meet administrative objectives
Plan and control research and operational budgets
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in social sciences, law, business administration, or public administration
Several years of experience in government policy development, research, or program administration
Senior public service roles may require security clearance
Extensive management experience is typically required
Leadership, governance expertise, and strong knowledge of public administration systems significantly enhance eligibility.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $73,100 to $145,900, depending on jurisdiction and seniority.

Leadership roles in legislative and administrative governance
